title: Devpilot P8 server-agent task-pull hardening
source_evidence: sampled source line counts for current dirty task-pull files

owns: finish sync into linked business runs
does_not_own: Prisma schema changes without separate approval

owns: local command execution and cancellation behavior
CLI summaries and process exit signaling

does_not_own: source refactors outside documented evidence needs

dependency_rules: CLI consumes task-pull contract; API does not depend on CLI internals.
API lifecycle endpoints preserve auth/default-off gates before job mutation.
Finish sync depends on terminal job outcome metadata, not on local CLI process details.
Docs and verification record evidence after behavior is implemented and checked.
risks: API and CLI can drift on finish/ack/result metadata shape.
Default-off server-agent task-pull gates must not be accidentally widened.
Line-count cleanup can hide behavior changes if refactors are too broad.
Docs can claim readiness before real long-connection or multi-instance gaps are closed.
